input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

recent_thinks: [0-2] The scene shifts from a solid, muted brownish-grey screen to a light pink background. Centered on the new background, three lines of text appear in a casual, light purple cursive font. The text explicitly reads "Bonnie Has A Spa Day From Dog About Town". This text display replaces the previous solid color entirely within the frame.

[2-4] The background shifts from a light pink hue to a smooth grey gradient. Centered purple text "Bonnie Has A Spa Day From Dog About Town" persists unchanged in font and position. Small, blurry white specks become visible near the bottom of the frame against the new grey backdrop, adding subtle atmospheric detail while the title remains static.

[4-6] The purple title disappears, replaced by a photo of a small tan and white dog standing on a dark surface. Tattooed human hands hold the dog's paw near a grooming tool. White text reading "Such an adorable face you have Bonnie!" overlays the bottom. The dog photo vanishes, leaving the grey background with only the white caption.

samples.output: <think>Grey background and caption vanish, replaced by new footage of a small tan dog standing in a white bathing tub. A purple leash extends upward from the neck area. A visible human arm in a dark short sleeve holds the dog steady as the animal looks downward into the tub.</think><action>silent</action>

You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).
